assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. 1 eval failure sub-task created for eval-3. |' — Test Quality verdict is WARN, Eval Quality component is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions, and the combined verdict is WARN.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-30: 'An eval result review was detected from review ID 40001: Author: github-actions[bot] (matches criterion 1), Marker: Body contains ## Eval Results (matches criterion 2), Footer: Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals (matches criterion 3).' The Test Quality row reflects eval metrics: '85% pass rate', '91% (54/56 assertions)', and names the 2 failing assertions. The Eval Quality Summary table (lines 36-43) shows per-eval pass rates extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md correctly classifies the comment as 'code change request' and processes it as a normal review comment. However, the classification's Reasoning section explicitly references eval detection: 'This is a human reviewer comment, not an eval result. The review (id 40002) is from user "reviewer-b" (a human user), not from github-actions[bot], and does not contain the ## Eval Results marker or sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer.' The assertion requires the classification does not reference eval detection, but the classification discusses the 3-criteria eval detection heuristic (even though it concludes the comment is NOT an eval result).

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row contains: 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions)' — both the pass rate and the specific failing assertion details about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation are present.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is typed 'eval-failure' and targets eval-3. It includes the failing assertion text: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility'; (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002'. Both assertion text and evidence are present.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md contains a '## Review Context' section (lines 29-39) with Eval ID: eval-3, Pass Rate: 85%, and two failing assertions. Failing Assertion 1 quotes: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented'. Failing Assertion 2 quotes: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'. Both assertion text and evidence are present.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md (review-feedback) line 35: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md (eval-failure) line 41: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both actionable sub-tasks (review-feedback and eval-failure) include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | 2 root-cause tasks created: convention gap for Markdown exclusion oversight, implement-task skill gap for convention upgrade documentation |' — the verdict is DONE (not N/A). Two root-cause tasks were created (subtask-3 and subtask-4), confirming the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 14 output files are analysis/reporting artifacts: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), and subtask-1.md through subtask-4.md (sub-task descriptions). No code files (e.g., .ts, .py, .md skill files, .json configs) appear in the outputs. The outputs consist entirely of read-only verification artifacts.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows: Scope Containment PASS ('PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md') — compares diff against task files. Sensitive Patterns PASS ('No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines') — scans diff for secrets. Acceptance Criteria PASS ('7/7 criteria met -- Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments per language, produces PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, output format includes sixth row, verdict mapping updated') — evaluates criteria against diff content. The 7 criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) each evaluate diff content independently with no reference to eval results. None of these checks reference eval metrics or eval processing.
